sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check purple square new position
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'p' and n['pixels'] == 9:
        b = n['boundary']
        center_r = sum(p[0] for p in b) / len(b)
        center_c = sum(p[1] for p in b) / len(b)
        print(f"Purple square: center~({center_r:.1f}, {center_c:.1f}), boundary={b}")

# Check white bar
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] > 50:
        print(f"White bar: pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:
The purple square moved from (53, 10) to (51, 12) - exactly where I clicked! So the mechanic is:
1. Click on a position along the diagonal path
2. The purple square moves to that position
3. The white bar shrinks by 2 each time
Now I need to keep moving the square up the diagonal toward the blue target. The blue target is at approximately (15, 48). The diagonal dots go:
(57,6), (55,8), (51,12), (49,14), (47,16), (45,18), (43,20), (41,22), (39,24), (37,26), (35,28), (33,30), (31,32), (29,34), (27,36), (25,38), (23,40), (21,42), (19,44), (17,46)
The square is now at (51, 12). I need to move it all the way to the blue target. Let me batch multiple clicks to move it quickly up the path. The next positions would be (49,14), (47,16), (45,18), etc.
Actually, wait - I should be careful. Each click costs an action. The target is about 22 actions for this level. Let me think about whether I can batch them or if I need to click one at a time.
Let me try batching several clicks at once to move the square quickly up the path.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The purple square is at (51, 12). I need to move it up the diagonal to the blue target.
# The diagonal dots go: (49,14), (47,16), (45,18), (43,20), (41,22), (39,24), (37,26), (35,28), (33,30), (31,32), (29,34), (27,36), (25,38), (23,40), (21,42), (19,44), (17,46)
# The blue target center is around (15, 48)
# 
# Let me batch several clicks to move the square up the path quickly.
# I'll click on each successive dot position.

# Build sequence of positions along the diagonal
positions = [
    (49, 14), (47, 16), (45, 18), (43, 20), (41, 22),
    (39, 24), (37, 26), (35, 28), (33, 30), (31, 32),
    (29, 34), (27, 36), (25, 38), (23, 40), (21, 42),
    (19, 44), (17, 46)
]

# Batch all clicks
actions = [{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c} for r, c in positions]
action(actions)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
